title: Monorepo scaffold with Tauri v2 + React
goal: Create a pnpm monorepo with Tauri v2 desktop app. Structure: apps/desktop (React + Tauri), packages/domain (shared TS types), packages/state (Zustand stores). Set up: pnpm-workspace.yaml, root package.json, tsconfig base, apps/desktop with Vite + React + TypeScript, apps/desktop/src-tauri with Cargo.toml and minimal Rust main. The app should compile and show a blank React page via 'cargo tauri dev'. Include vitest config at root. Do NOT populate domain/state packages beyond package.json + tsconfig — other packets handle that.
acceptance_criteria: vitest.config.ts exists at root or workspace level

title: Core domain type definitions
goal: Create @composecraft/domain with core TypeScript types. Define: Project (id, name, createdAt, updatedAt, schemaVersion), Asset (id, name, type, layerId, data), Layer (id, name, visible, locked, order, assets), Command (id, type, payload, timestamp), CommandResult (success, error?, data?), PanelId (union type: 'sidebar'|'inspector'|'canvas'|'bottom'), WorkspaceLayout (panels visibility map). Export all from index.ts. Add basic type tests.
acceptance_criteria: At least one type test file

forbidden_rationale.apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs: Protected: command registration owned by integration packet

title: Rust command registry skeleton
goal: Create the Rust command module structure in apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/. Add: commands/ module dir with mod.rs, a ping.rs command that returns a structured JSON response, and a shared error type (AppError with code/message/hint). The ping command should accept an optional message string and return {pong: true, message: string, timestamp: string}. Add cargo tests for the ping command. Do NOT modify lib.rs (forbidden) — the integration packet registers commands there.

forbidden_rationale.apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs: Protected: command registration owned by integration packet

title: Zustand store architecture
goal: Create @composecraft/state with Zustand stores. Build three stores: workspaceStore (panel visibility, active panel, layout), projectStore (current project metadata, loading state, dirty flag), commandStore (command history array, last command, canUndo/canRedo booleans). Each store in its own file. Export all from index.ts. Use @composecraft/domain types. Add tests for each store covering initial state, mutations, and reset.
acceptance_criteria: projectStore with project metadata + dirty flag

forbidden_rationale.apps/desktop/src/App.tsx: Protected: root layout owned by integration packet

title: React workspace layout shell
goal: Create the React workspace layout with 4 panel areas: sidebar (left, collapsible), canvas (center, fills remaining space), inspector (right, collapsible), bottom panel (bottom, collapsible, for logs/jobs). Use workspaceStore to control panel visibility. Each panel should be a placeholder component showing its name. Add a top toolbar with panel toggle buttons. Style with CSS modules or inline styles (no external UI library). Wire the invoke('ping') call to a button in the toolbar as proof of Tauri command round-trip.
acceptance_criteria: Ping button calls invoke('ping') and shows response

forbidden_rationale.apps/desktop/src/App.tsx: Protected: root layout owned by integration packet
forbidden_rationale.apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs: Protected: command registration owned by integration packet

title: Phase 1 integration wiring
goal: Wire everything together: register ping command in lib.rs generate_handler! macro, update App.tsx to render the Workspace component, ensure domain and state package exports are correct, verify the app compiles and launches with cargo tauri dev. Fix any import path issues between packages.
acceptance_criteria: No TypeScript or Rust compilation errors

title: Architecture documentation
goal: Write docs/architecture.md documenting: monorepo structure and package responsibilities, command flow (UI → invoke → Rust command → response → store update), state architecture (3 Zustand stores and their roles), panel system (4 panels, visibility controlled by workspaceStore), protected files and why they're protected, seam files and integration rules. Keep it concise and accurate to what Phase 1 actually built.
